name: Jinyu Chinese

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Jinyu Chinese (jiny1235).

notes: Skeleton. Jin (Jinyu) is a Sinitic variety of Shanxi and neighbouring areas, written in Han (Chinese) characters. Han script is logographic: a character's reading is a lexical fact, not composable from graphic parts, so grapheme-to-IPA requires a per-word pronunciation stratum (a character-to-reading lexicon for this specific variety) that the engine does not bundle. Without that stratum engine coverage is effectively zero, so graphemes are left empty rather than faked. Lexical tone, the distinctive glottal-stop checked syllables, and tone sandhi are not recoverable from the characters.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Jinyu Chinese (jiny1235)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
